extracted_information: The web content introduces 'Message Delta Compression' as a bandwidth-efficient real-time messaging strategy by Ably. This technique reduces the bandwidth required to transmit real-time messages by sending only the changes (deltas) from the previous message to subscribers, instead of the entire message. This optimizes bandwidth, improves latencies, and simplifies development by handling message size optimization.

specifications.diff_algorithm: Ably's proprietary diff algorithm, based on open VCDIFF format for delta generation and open source xDelta algorithm for decoding.

message_size_limits.delta_payload_example: If a 64KiB message has only 4KiB of differing content, only a 4KiB delta message is sent.

features.name: Message Delta Compression
features.description: Sends only changes (deltas) between messages instead of full messages, significantly reducing bandwidth.
benefits: Simplifies development by abstracting message size management.
features.how_it_works: Publisher sends the entire message. Ably's diff algorithm encodes the difference (delta) between the new and previous payload, and sends this delta to subscribers. Deltas are applied only to the principal payload, not metadata.
scenarios_for_full_message_delivery: First message received when a client subscribes to a channel.
First message upon reconnecting after disconnection or being dropped.
When generating a delta does not result in significant (>20%) bandwidth reduction.
integration_methods: Ably Client Library SDKs (v1.2+): Specify parameters on a per-channel basis via `ChannelOptions` API.

Non-Ably libraries (e.g., MQTT, Server-Sent Events): Publishers still send full messages to Ably (encoding handled by Ably), but subscribers must decode deltas themselves using provided VCDIFF decoder libraries. Requires using channel qualifiers.

features.name: State Persistence
features.description: Ability to persist the last real-time message on a channel for 365 days. Useful for channels with varying or low message frequency (e.g., dashboard showing market data).
features.how_to_enable: Enable 'Persist last message' on Channel rules in Ably dashboard.
features.retrieval: Achieved using `rewind=1` on the rewind API.

statistics.bandwidth_savings_example: A 16KiB initial message followed by a 4KiB additional data update results in sending only 4KiB with delta compression, instead of 20KiB (16KiB + 4KiB) without.
statistics.tennis_match_example: A 64KiB message where only 4KiB differs from the previous one, will send only 4KiB with delta compression, instead of 64KiB.

temporal_info.message_delta_compression_release: Available as part of Client Library SDKs v1.2 release (published around June 2020, based on image date).

references: https://ably.com/blog/practical-guide-to-diff-algorithms (Practical Guide to Diff Algorithms and Patch File Formats)
https://ably.com/dashboard (Ably dashboard for bandwidth savings info)
